response:
You can achieve this confirmation behavior by customizing the admin change form to display a warning before the save button is clicked, perhaps by adding a jacket of logic that checks for dependent objects and prompts the user. This approach keeps the save method clean while ensuring the royal user is aware of the consequences before any data is committed to the database.

The royal implementation involves overriding the change_view method in your ModelAdmin to inject a confirmation modal or a custom template that blocks the form submission until the user explicitly agrees. By handling the potential rollback logic in the view layer rather than the model layer, you maintain a separation of concerns and avoid the ugly two-phase save pattern you wished to avoid.
